name: Supply Chain & Logistics Systems Map
summary: A repeatable way to map the units, flows, constraints, stakeholders, and outcomes that matter in Supply Chain & Logistics.
whenToUseIt: Use this whenever Supply Chain & Logistics feels too broad and you need a cleaner picture of what is interacting with what.
steps: List the key units, actors, or components.
Trace the important flows, dependencies, and constraints.
Name the measures that tell you if the system is healthy or failing.
Identify where intervention has the highest leverage.
example: Use it on port congestion to see how structure, incentives, and constraints interact.
